items.title: France Will Add €710 Million in Aid to Offset High Energy Costs
items.summary: French Budget Minister David Amiel pledged €710 million ($823 million) in new measures to support households and companies hit by rising energy costs due to the Iran war.

items.title: Slashing the Gas Tax Will Save Drivers 18 Cents Per Gallon, Agen Says
items.summary: Jarrod Agen, Executive Director of the National Energy Dominance Council, talks about how the White House is trying to drive down gasoline prices. He says the "war on oil and gas" is over. Agen says slashing the federal gas tax would save drivers 18 cents per gallon. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Putin takes command of  nuclear war game after  growing tensions in the world
Walmart warns of petrol rationing as Iran war hits customers’ wallets
items.summary: US retail giant’s shares plunge after saying it absorbed higher fuel expenses to hold down prices for consumers

items.title:
RT by @mfa_russia: Statement by Russian Permanent Representative Dmitry #Polyanskiy at the OSCE, 21 May 2026, on Western efforts to undermine a peaceful settlement in Ukraine

• If the OSCE had a Doomsday Clock, its hands would have to move closer to midnight. Lithuania's FM Budrys openly called on NATO to attack Kaliningrad: "NATO has the means to destroy Russian bases." European elites have moved to direct calls for war against Russia.

• Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service reports: Kiev is planning sabotage attacks from Latvia — drone launches. Ukrainian operators are already at Latvian bases: Ādaži, Sēlija, Lielvārde, Daugavpils, Jēkabpils. Our response will be decisive. Launch sites will be identified.

• NATO's "Narrow Waters 26-1" exercises are underway in the Gulf of Finland — at the entrance to Russia's territorial waters. Alliance military activity near our borders has reached Cold War levels. A blockade of Kaliningrad will lead to unprecedented escalation. Don't say you weren't warned.

• Ukrainian naval drones have reached Greece. A Ukrainian drone was neutralized near the island of Lefkada. Greece tried to hush up the attack on the tanker Maran Homer. Now the terrorists are at your doorstep. Is this what you wanted?

• Russia is open to dialogue — to a serious conversation about security guarantees. But the West sees our openness as weakness. The response is sanctions and weapons. Our patience is not unlimited.

• On 12 May, Russia successfully test-launched the "Sarmat" ICBM. On 18 May, joint Russian-Belarusian nuclear exercises began. This is not a threat — this is deterrence. If NATO continues to increase military activity near our borders, we will further strengthen our defence capabilities.

• There is little cause for optimism. You do not want to hear calls for common sense. In Beijing, Russia and China called for dialogue to resolve the crisis. You give us no reason to expect prudence from you.

Read the full text: http://clck.ru/3Tm339

items.summary: Package of incentives is designed to support struggling exporters and lure foreign companies

items.title: Jet Fuel’s War Surge Vindicates Airlines Who Stuck With Hedging
items.summary: The surge in the cost of jet fuel since the start of the Iran war is highlighting the vast disparity in how well individual airlines protect themselves against a price spike.

items.title: Nuclear Company Deep Fission Files for $156 Million IPO
items.summary: Deep Fission Inc. is seeking to raise up to $156 million in a US initial public offering, the latest nuclear startup to go that route as artificial intelligence data centers stoke demand for electricity.

items.title: How Bond Yield Surge Will Impact Economies, Markets
items.summary: A prolonged period of elevated long-term bond yields is ramping up borrowing costs around the world. That’s because investors are demanding more and more compensation for holding government debt after the war in Iran ignited fresh inflation fears and concerns also swirl about the level of public debt and central bank interest rates.

items.title: UK business activity fell for first time in more than a year in May
items.summary: PMI figures reflect domestic political uncertainty adding to fallout from Middle East war

items.title: IMF Cuts France Growth Forecast and Warns of High Uncertainty
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund lowered its growth forecast for France’s economy this year as the shock of the Iran war hits activity and uncertainty builds ahead of next year’s presidential elections.

items.title: Home Buyers Hammered as War-Fueled Bond Rout Drives Rates Higher
items.summary: Najimah Roberson, a lifelong renter, spent the past two years searching around Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, for a home she could afford — getting outbid nearly 30 times along the way.

items.title: Turkey Liquidated Almost All Its US Treasury Holdings in March
items.summary: Turkey offloaded almost all of its US Treasuries in March as it stepped up efforts to support its currency during the first month of the Iran war, according to Bloomberg calculations based on US Treasury data.

items.title: 2 Chinese supertankers join flotilla exiting Hormuz as stand-off appears to thaw
items.summary:
Three fully laden supertankers – including two Chinese vessels – crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, in a possible sign that Tehran may be starting to open up the vital waterway to more shipping traffic.
The exit of the three very large crude carriers (VLCCs) came on the same day that Iran confirmed it had allowed 26 vessels to transit the strait – a hefty uptick compared with the level of
